trajectory.description: Observation: eval failed due to torch.load weights_only=True. Training accuracy ~0.01 (worse than chance 0.016), loss exploding from 4.2 to 300+, indicating learning rate too high / features not normalized. Analysis: linear head on frozen CLIP features diverges; 1e-3 lr likely too high for high-dimensional features. Decision: fix predict.py with weights_only=False, add LayerNorm before classifier, lower lr to 1e-4, add gradient clipping 1.0, reorder DataParallel wrapping. Retrain 5 epochs, submit eval for new baseline.

trajectory.description: Observation: Training loss becomes NaN after ~500 steps, val worst-region accuracy 0.0007 (near zero). Train/val acc never above random. Analysis: Linear probe with lr=1e-4 causes weight explosion, possibly due to unscaled features despite LayerNorm. Need to lower lr, add warmup, and enable fine-tuning to improve feature quality. Decision: rewrite model.py with unfreeze_last_n option, train.py with lr=1e-5, 500-step warmup, cosine annealing, AMP, unfreeze last 2 ViT layers, train 10 epochs, submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: train.py has UnboundLocalError because train_dataset referenced before assignment; iter 2 training had NaN loss with lr=1e-4 linear probe despite LayerNorm. Analysis: need to fix ordering, and linear probe alone may be insufficient; fine-tuning whole vision with low lr and warmup can improve. Decision: rewrite train.py with proper ordering, use CLIPVisionModel (not full CLIPModel) to save memory, enable full vision fine-tuning with lr=2e-5, warmup+cosine schedule, gradient clipping, AMP, and fix model.py accordingly. Train 10 epochs, then submit eval.

Observation: NaN loss at step 0 with current train.py (finetune_vision=True, lr=2e-5, AMP, gradient clipping). Analysis: possible instability from mixed precision, high feature magnitude, or DataParallel. Decision: rewrite train.py to be minimal and stable: no AMP, lr=1e-5, weight_decay=0, gradient clipping disabled, drop_last=True, warmup 300 steps, cosine decay, and sanity check before training. Also remove DataParallel for V1 to ensure stability, using single GPU (can scale later). Train 10 epochs, submit eval to get baseline worst_region_accuracy.

trajectory.description: Observation: Baseline worst_region_accuracy 0.3999 with ViT-B fine-tuned 10 epochs, single GPU. Analysis: ViT-B capacity may be limiting; main bottleneck is sub-Saharan Africa accuracy. Need larger model (ViT-L) with multi-GPU training, stronger augmentation, and class-balanced sampling to improve generalization across regions. Decision: Upgrade to ViT-L/14, use 4 GPUs (DataParallel), stronger augmentation (RandAugment), class-weighted sampler, train 25 epochs, log per-region val accuracy to select best checkpoint, then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 8 training crashed with NaN loss at step 1, likely due to unstable ViT-L initialization with lr=2e-5 and AMP. Iter 6 ViT-B baseline achieved 0.3999 worst-region (Africa worst). Analysis: ViT-L is higher capacity but training instability wastes iteration. Better approach: stick with ViT-B (stable), add Group DRO (Distributed Robust Optimization) to directly optimize worst-region accuracy by upweighting high-loss regions, along with class-weighted sampling for class imbalance. Group DRO is proven for WILDS tasks. Decision: rewrite train.py with Group DRO, strong augmentation, 4-GPU DataParallel, 20 epochs, log per-region val accuracy, save best checkpoint based on worst-region val accuracy. Then submit eval.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 9 crashed with 'backward through the graph a second time' due to flawed Group DRO implementation (multiple backward calls per step). Previous best eval: 0.3999 worst-region (Africa) with ViT-B 10 epochs single-GPU. Time budget 6.5h, 21 iterations left. Analysis: Need stable Group DRO to directly optimize worst-region accuracy. Must fix backward to compute weighted loss once per step and update group weights (q) correctly. ViT-B fine-tuning with 4 GPUs, class-balanced sampler, and strong augmentation should improve Africa accuracy. Decision: Rewrite train.py with correct Group DRO, per-sample weighting, q-update avoided gradients. Use ViT-B (stable), 30 epochs, 4-GPU DataParallel, cosine schedule, grad clipping, log per-region val accuracy, save best checkpoint based on worst-region val accuracy. Submit eval after training.

trajectory.description: Observation: Iter 10 training (Group DRO attempt) produced NaN loss from epoch 0, wasted 2.5h; best eval so far is 0.3999 worst-region (ViT-B, 10 epochs, single GPU). Analysis: Complex approaches caused instability; need a reliable, stable training that can improve within remaining 4h and 20 iterations. Decision: Return to ViT-B fine-tuning (stable), use 4 GPUs for faster iterations, strong augmentation, class-weighted sampling, label smoothing, 30 epochs, log per-region val accuracy, save best checkpoint by worst-region, then submit eval. This should raise worst-region accuracy above 0.3999 safely.
